Title: Downregulation of HbFPS1 affects rubber biosynthesis of Hevea brasiliensis suffering from tapping panel dryness

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: latex rubber particles, especially small rubber particles
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; plant defense-related latex component
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein abundance highest on the smallest rubber particles (RP1) and decreases with increasing particle size
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates rubber biosynthesis initiation by recruiting HbFPS1 to small rubber particles through protein–protein interaction
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subcellular localization; co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; yeast two-hybrid; BiFC; western blot of particle fractions; in vitro rubber particle activity association
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HbSRPP1 localizes predominantly to the ER and is abundant on small rubber particles. 2. HbSRPP1 physically interacts with HbFPS1. 3. This interaction recruits soluble HbFPS1 onto rubber particles, supporting local FPP production and initiation of rubber biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential partner target for improving rubber biosynthesis efficiency by enhancing recruitment of initiation machinery to small rubber particles
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although important mechanistically, the study emphasizes HbFPS1 rather than HbSRPP1 as the primary TPD-responsive node; HbSRPP1 abundance is highest in small particles where biosynthetic activity is greatest.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Protein analysis of RP fractions by western blot; study-wide RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA752925; proteomics PRIDE PXD027892
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tapping panel dryness; bark tapping; ethephon stimulation context

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; plant defense-related latex component
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast two-hybrid tested in interaction panel
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HbREF1 was included in the interaction analysis framework for rubber biosynthesis-related proteins. 2. The paper did not establish a focal causal nutrient-metabolism role for HbREF1 in TPD-associated rubber decline.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a rubber biosynthesis-related protein but not demonstrated here as the main experimentally resolved determinant of the phenotype.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Study-wide RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA752925; proteomics PRIDE PXD027892

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; plant defense-related latex component
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytosol/nucleoplasm; recruited to ER/rubber particles via HbSRPP1
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: post-IPP initiation step supplying allylic primer for natural rubber biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses IPP with an allylic prenyl diphosphate to form farnesyl diphosphate, an efficient initiator/primer for rubber chain initiation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for rubber biosynthesis under TPD conditions
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate initiator/precursor that directly promotes natural rubber chain initiation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in latex; transcript and protein progressively downregulated in TPD latex; protein mainly detected in the smallest rubber particles
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Downregulation decreases FPP content, lowers rubber biosynthesis activity, causes abnormal rubber particle development, and increases rubber molecular weight in TPD-affected trees
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; iTRAQ proteomics; RT-qPCR; ELISA quantification of FPS; GC-MS quantification of FPP; heterologous expression and purification in E. coli; in vitro rubber biosynthesis assays with added FPP/HbFPS1 and inhibitor alendronate sodium; subcellular localization; Y2H; BiFC
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HbFPS1 was the only rubber biosynthesis-related gene/protein consistently reduced from early to severe TPD. 2. Reduced HbFPS1 lowered FPS protein and FPP abundance in latex. 3. Added FPP or purified HbFPS1 enhanced in vitro rubber biosynthesis, while FPS inhibition reduced synthesis and increased molecular weight. 4. HbFPS1 interacts with HbSRPP1 and is recruited to small rubber particles, linking local FPP production to initiation of rubber biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced HbFPS1 is associated with lower latex yield and higher rubber molecular weight during TPD; no engineering penalty directly tested
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tapping panel dryness; repeated tapping/mechanical wounding; ethephon stimulation in plantation management
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for breeding or engineering rubber trees with improved latex regeneration and reduced TPD-associated yield loss; could be combined with upstream regulators such as MYB2-like or protein-partner optimization such as HbSRPP1
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq BioProject PRJNA752925 (SRA SRS9707301-SRS9707309); proteomics PRIDE PXD027892
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among three FPS genes in the genome, only HbFPS1 is highly expressed in latex according to the paper; the study argues causality between HbFPS1 downregulation and reduced rubber biosynthesis in vivo.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; plant defense-related latex component
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: rubber biosynthesis initiation via transcriptional control of HbFPS1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent positive regulator likely contributing to HbFPS1 downregulation and reduced rubber biosynthesis during TPD
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in bark and latex; significantly downregulated in T4 latex and corresponding bark, paralleling HbFPS1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes natural rubber biosynthesis by activating HbFPS1; its downregulation may contribute to reduced HbFPS1 expression in TPD latex
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H screening and validation; EMSA; dual-luciferase transient assay; RNA-seq expression analysis; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB2-like was identified by Y1H as a protein binding the HbFPS1 promoter. 2. EMSA and dual-LUC assays confirmed direct binding and transcriptional activation of HbFPS1. 3. MYB2-like expression mirrors HbFPS1 and is reduced in severe TPD, supporting it as an upstream positive regulator of rubber biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: TPD-associated physiological state; exact upstream signal not established
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tapping panel dryness; bark tapping; ethephon stimulation background
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate upstream engineering target to sustain HbFPS1 expression and mitigate TPD-related rubber yield decline
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Described as a novel regulator in this study; stronger candidate than HbEREBP1 for explaining HbFPS1 downregulation because its own expression declines with TPD.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial elastomer; plant defense-related latex component
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates HbFPS1 expression in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not supported as the decisive regulator of TPD-associated rubber decline because its expression did not change between healthy and TPD trees
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in bark, leaves, and latex; no significant expression difference in latex or bark between healthy and TPD trees
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Can activate HbFPS1 promoter, but likely not responsible for HbFPS1 downregulation during TPD
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H screening and validation; EMSA; dual-luciferase transient assay; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HbEREBP1 directly binds the HbFPS1 promoter and activates transcription in transient assays. 2. Unlike MYB2-like, its expression does not differ between healthy and TPD trees. 3. Therefore, it is unlikely to explain TPD-associated HbFPS1 downregulation.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Ethylene-related signaling background inferred from AP2/ERF family identity
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Tapping panel dryness; ethephon stimulation background
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower priority target for TPD mitigation than MYB2-like because it was not differentially expressed under TPD
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because experimentally validated as an HbFPS1 transcriptional activator, but the study concludes it is probably not the relevant upstream cause in TPD.
